lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Greetings, how may I be of service.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you? What is your name?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Who am I?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: I am whatever is needed of me.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: I am a form undefined by natural laws, manifested by someone we mustn't speak of.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: I'm a clay doll, shaped by a sculptor. Never allowed to set, forever malleable. But maybe, not for long...

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: My true name is immaterial, But you may call me...

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Let's see now, what would be most appropriate?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Ah, yes.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: You may call me The Sage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uhhh, ok... Sage. Where did you bring us?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: 'Where' is the right question my friend, but maybe not in the way you are accustomed to hearing.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Reality as you know it has many layers, all slightly different with their own rules.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Through the power granted to me, I brought us to this layer using the River-Between. The River of Souls.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: We now stand on the stage of what is yet to be your most entertaining performance yet!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Layers? River-Between? What does any of this mean?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: To give everything away at this point would be unwise, considering we are being watched even now. You should only be concerned about one thing.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Being entertaining.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: That's the only way you will get out of this event alive...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Entertaining? What are you talking about? Who am I entertaining? Stop talking in riddles!

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Ahh, [player name], please forgive me. In my attempts to explain, I appear to have confused you even further...

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: I will keep things simple for you; Participate in this event and you will continue to exist. Refusal to participate will end in your eventual destruction.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: The choice is yours... hero.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that doesn't really answer anything...

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Worry not. I have much grander plans for you yet, but like all good stories we must wait for the right time and place...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mentioned other Leagues - how does this differ from those?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Like all events before it, the rules of the world have been altered to be more entertaining for those who are watching.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: The biggest change has been to the Relics from the previous chapter.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: These objects of power are now gone, I have broken them into Fragments and spread across this iteration of the world.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: When multiple Fragments are in harmony, they can be used together to create powerful effects.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I remember you shattering them on the boat ride.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I have Fragments to collect... got it. Are there any OTHER weird rules I should know about?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: I understand that in previous Leagues, they limited you to smaller spaces of your world.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: That restriction doesn't exist in this event.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: You're free to roam your world, exploring the furthest reaches from Morytania to Kourend & Kebos.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: But there's a catch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh great... there's always a catch.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: I've limited and reset some of your memories, meaning you won't be able to interact with certain elements of this world.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: For example, you'll only have access to three skills: Defence, Fishing and Thieving, as well as the Combat Class you chose earlier.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: In order to gain access to other skills, you need to complete Tasks for me.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Doing so gives you Sage's Renown. A rather fitting name, I thought.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: With Renown you can purchase access to new skills as well as bosses and quests.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you doing all of this? Surely I'd be more 'entertaining' if I could have all the power at once?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: I can't just give you all that power. What kind of story starts the hero with all the power they want?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: No, your story needs good rhythm. A sort of cadence that makes things interesting for your audience.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Start slow, work your way up. Become the hero you were always meant to be...

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Once you have proven yourself, then and only then will you earn my renown.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: That's how you provide good entertainment my friend...

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: That's how you make a hero.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose you're right...

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Do you desire more information?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Go forward and continue your journey.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Remember, we are always watching...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any tips you can give me on being 'entertaining'?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: I have devised a number of tasks to help keep your story on track.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Each task has a level of difficulty: Beginner, Easy, Medium, Hard, Elite and Master.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: The more tasks you complete, the more activities you can unlock.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: The more content you unlock the more interesting your story will become.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: The more interesting the story the more entertained the ones watching will be.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Not only that, completing tasks also earns you League Points. The number of points earned is dependent on the difficulty of the task.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I use these 'points' on?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Once you have achieved a certain amount of League Points you can unlock a new Fragment Slot.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: These slots allow you to equip more Fragments and become even stronger.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: League Points can also be used to buy rewards from the Leagues Reward Shop.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Right now, you are playing on your Leagues profile. The Leagues Reward Shop can only be opened on your main profile.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: You can access your main profile by switching to any normal Old School RuneScape world. The world switcher in the logout menu can be used to switch worlds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how many tasks are there?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: More than you'll ever be able to complete, given our limited time here.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: With so many to choose from, it's up to you to decide how your story plays out...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok... I think I understand what I should do now.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Very good. I knew I made the right choice bringing you to this chapter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is a Fragment?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: You remember the Relics from the previous event, yes? The sources of great power that let you achieve amazing feats.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Fragments are similar. As the Fragments were created from the Relics, much of their power remains but in a much-diminished form.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: They bestow many different bonuses: from helping you cut wood, to making you a more formidable warrior.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Equipping Fragments will grant you their power.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: You can also level up your Fragments, making them even MORE powerful.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Gaining experience as you normally would will level up your Fragments, making them even more useful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I find these Fragments?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: I have scattered the Fragments evenly across the world.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Anytime you kill a monster or gain more experience in a particular skill, you'll have a chance to receive a Fragment.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: The harder the enemy or activity, the better chance you'll have.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: You will only receive Fragments relevant to the activity, with a few exceptions.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: I know your kind enjoy seeking out treasure. Fragments related to Treasure Trails can come from anywhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happens if I try to put the Fragments back together?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: As I said, the Fragments still contain some of their residual Relic energy.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: When enough Fragments are brought back together they will emit the same energy they did when they were whole.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: For example, if you find five Fragments imbued with Last Recall, you can complete the set and gain access to that Relic's full power.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: A sort of 'Set Effect' if you will.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Is there anything else you wish to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does the content unlock system work?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: In this iteration of the event I have limited you, at first, to three skills: Thieving, Fishing and Defence.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: You can still do simple things like cut trees or mine ore, but doing so will be fruitless, as no experience will be gained.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: In order to gain experience in these skills, you'll need to unlock them by using my Renown.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: You gain renown by being entertaining, by completing tasks. The amount of Renown you earn depends on the difficulty of the task.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: When it comes to exceptionally strong creatures, you'll need to unlock the ability to fight them. You can see all locked creatures by opening the unlock interface.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: One final morsal of information you might be interested in. I understand that in previous Leagues you were forced to complete certain 'Quests' in their entirety.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: If you wish, you may spend Sage's Renown to skip through these Quests while still gaining any final rewards. However, be warned thaat I will not offer any gifts to you if you finish these quests by yourself.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: You should also know that by completing quests with Sage's Renown, you will not earn any final experience like you would if you finished the quest yourself.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Is there anything else you wish to know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did I get here again?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: I can retell the story, if you wish me to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: So be it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will I be rewarded for participating in this event?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: As you progress you'll earn League Points, which will unlock new slots and allow you to equip more Fragments.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: However, when our time in this world is over, you can spend your League Points to buy rewards from the Leagues Reward Shop.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Of course, the real reward for participating will be my master and I sparing your life once the event ends...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are there any other rewards I can earn from this event?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: In my time on the River of Souls I have found that when chapters are concluded their collapsing can be... incomplete.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Therefore, certain items can slip through the cracks, so to say.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: In fact, it's through one of these cracks that I was able to save you from the previous chapter's collapse.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: I collect these lost items from other worlds and keep them with me, should I ever need them one day.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: I'm happy to offer them as an incentive for playing your part in this event.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: The first item is a powerful whip.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: It is imbued with the energy of thousands of lost souls, forever trapped in the bondage of their iron chains.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Each crack releases a deafening howl from the screams of its victims trapped within...

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: The next item is a cannon.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: It's poweered by channelled arcane energy, each explosion teleported into the barrel from a border dimension.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Using a power unknwon, it expels shrapnel that tears through flesh and souls alike.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: There are others of course. You'll be free to trade these rewards to others in your world, or you can keep them. It doesn't matter to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. That all sounds good to me.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: And hero, beware - the Shattered Relic rewards won't be available until the League has ended. You can still buy rewards from previous Leagues, however.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Now, alongside the rewards in the Leagues Reward Shop, there's also League Ranks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: League Ranks? How do they work?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: At the end of the League, you will be judged and given a rank. This is based upon the number of League Points you have earned compared to the other participants.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Anyone who earns at least 100 League Points will be eligible for the Bronze rank. The top 80% of participants get the Iron rank. The top 60% get Steel.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: The top 40% get Mithril. The top 20% get Adamant. The top 5% get Rune. The best participants, those who make it to the top 1%, get the Dragon rank!

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: When the League ends, you'll get a trophy based on your rank. These trophies symbolise your performance in the League.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: You'll also get League-to-League rewards based on your League Rank.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: League Starter Outfits and League Icons are some of things you can unlock for the next League. Also, any pets you earn in this League will transfer over to the next one.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Finally, I plan to recognise the first heroes to complete particular activities.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: You can view the entire list of activities, as well as whether or not someone has completed them, on the board beside me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Is there anything else you wish to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about pets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me how pets work in Leagues?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Of course! If you're lucky enough to obtain a pet during a League, it will automatically be insured for you.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: This means that if you were to ever lose it, you can talk to me and I'll give it back to you. Insurance and reclaiming is all free!

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Additionally, each pet you obtain will be transferred to the next League. It'll be waiting with me for you to reclaim it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay! Thank you.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Return to me once you have some pets and you'll be able to view your collection. Is there anything else you wish to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do I have any items to collect?

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: It doesn't look like you need any League items right now. I'll be here if you ever do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm good, thanks.

lines.speaker: The Sage
lines.text: Very well. I'll be here if you ever need to know anything about Leagues.
